other_observers: When I left, I was the only one who had seen it. Two or three other birders were going to look for it around 11:30 A.M.; I found the bird. At the time of this writting, I was the only one I know saw it. No other birders there in the A.M.
specific_location: Rose Pond dam along the south side of the pond on Chico Basin Ranch
description: This part was easy. It was a warbler by high, thin song. The bright orange face with dark auricular was strongly noticeable as the bird finally came into view after having heard it sing for about five minutes. The throat was flame orange too. The wingbars seemed to merge to for a large white panel of white on a dark wing. It had a yellow orange washed breast with dark flank streaks. I didn't look much at anything else.Behaviors: Mostly sang, but when I finally saw it, it moved down from the tallest leaves to just above mid-tree height and foraged among the cottonwood seeds, apparently for insects there.Call: sang partial song about every 30-40 seconds - I didn't know it, but knew it was one of the four highest-pitched eastern warblers, and for sure not the stacato notes of Blackpoll Warbler.Plumage: alternate
